steps.title: Identify slope and y-intercept
steps.content:
For $y = 2x - 5$, we extract the slope and y-intercept from the slope-intercept form.
  • Slope: $m = 2$ (line rises 2 units for every 1 unit to the right)
  • Y-intercept: $b = -5$ (line crosses the y-axis at $(0, -5)$, which is 5 units below the origin)

steps.title: Find the matching graph
steps.content: Look for the graph that crosses the y-axis at $(0, -5)$ and has a positive slope of $2$. The line should rise 2 units for every 1 unit moved to the right. That matches choice D — D is correct.
